title: Login With Excessively Long Password Value Displays Appropriate Validation Error
description: Verifies that entering a password that far exceeds the maximum allowed length (e.g., 1000 characters) is handled gracefully — either via client-side inline validation or a controlled server-side error — without crashing the UI or exposing system internals.
expected_result: The application handles the oversized password input gracefully. Either client-side validation caps or flags the input, or a controlled error message is shown. No stack trace, 500 error, or application crash occurs. The user remains on the login page with a usable form.

test_steps.action: Fill the email field with a valid-format email address
Fill the password field with a 1000-character string
test_steps.expected: Password field is populated with the oversized string

test_steps.action: Wait for a validation or error response
test_steps.expected: Either an inline field error or a general error message is displayed — no application crash, stack trace, or 500 error page appears

test_steps.action: Verify the page is still functional and user remains on /login
test_steps.expected: URL is still /login; no crash; login form is still rendered and interactable
